title: "podman commit" fails with ZFS storage driver
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**


When using the ZFS storage driver, running **podman commit** fails with "read-only file system" error.

Possibly related:
https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3773
https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/416


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a container with Podman using the ZFS storage driver.
```
podman run -dti --name="testcontainer" --network="podnet0" localhost/rhel
```

2. Try to use "podman commit" to create an image of the container.
```
podman commit testcontainer localhost/testimage
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```

time="2020-04-19T03:09:51-04:00" level=debug msg="layer list: [\"92b864bfcfaa5486b735fd9bfe48cf59636532ce9873532bef2cf9988570c858\" \"14694d06ab05beda84e7a2f9403ce202fbd32078bf52553b4949c54101947954\" \"1dcb22451b7885bdcdf1f1126b95eb4115792b0d49a31196e6a3941b579817c4\" \"20885b1d7e17eac7c9934b398844ebfb17b08b4de4232a40e8aef8b1179813da\"]"

time="2020-04-19T03:09:51-04:00" level=debug msg="unmount(\"/nvme/containers/r/storage/zfs/graph/20885b1d7e17eac7c9934b398844ebfb17b08b4de4232a40e8aef8b1179813da\")" storage-driver=zfs
time="2020-04-19T03:09:51-04:00" level=error msg="error copying layers and metadata for container \"9eccc1a6226489d4b98385529a251d1674847029cc165733f17f9710082a0757\": Error initializing source containers-storage:testcontainer: error extracting layer \"20885b1d7e17eac7c9934b398844ebfb17b08b4de4232a40e8aef8b1179813da\": error modifying zfs mountpoint (/nvme/containers/r/storage/zfs/graph/1dcb22451b7885bdcdf1f1126b95eb4115792b0d49a31196e6a3941b579817c4) directory ownership: chown /nvme/containers/r/storage/zfs/graph/1dcb22451b7885bdcdf1f1126b95eb4115792b0d49a31196e6a3941b579817c4: read-only file system"
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
New image is created.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

- Running as root.
- Issue always occurs.
- It looks like "podman commit" is mounting read-only causing the error.
- Disabling SELinux doesn't help.
- Commenting out storage options in /etc/containers/storage.conf doesn't help

Overlay storage driver is also broken when podman is backed by ZFS. Because committing doesn't work when using the zfs driver, I'm forced to use the vfs driver.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.12
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

```

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical host running RHEL 8.1.
```
cat /etc/redhat-release
     Red Hat Enterprise Linux release 8.1 (Ootpa)

uname -r
     4.18.0-147.8.1.el8_1.x86_64

zfs version
     zfs-0.8.3-1
```

comments.body: Thanks for opening the issue! It looks to be similar (or the same) as https://github.com/containers/storage/issues/602. Note that ZFS is not support on RHEL.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
